programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Ethical Every Day
programme.desc: . Rising temperatures are changing marine life. Sushi chains introduce unfamiliar fish, and researchers seek to breed species that can endure higher temperatures. This time, living with a warming sea.

programme.title: Newsline Asia 24
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.desc: . In recent years, Kyoto Buddhist temples have fewer parishioners and are searching for ways to make Buddhist teachings relevant to modern times, ensuring the continuance of their places of worship. Gary Tegler, Takuji Takahashi, Ikuko Takeda, Sumie Nagakusa, Tomoko Eri, Toshiaki Nagakusa, Kokei Eri, Yoshiaki Dobuchi.

programme.title: Newsline Asia 24
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: . Our bodies are home to trillions of bacteria that influence our physical health and mental well-being. We explore Japan's relationship with fermented foods and "chokatsu" or gut-related activities. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Backstage Japan
programme.desc: . Visit one of Japan's engineering marvels, an auto and train bridge spanning six islands, and meet the dedicated crew working to keep it as good as new for the next 200 years.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
programme.desc: . Introducing asari, Japan's most popular clam. Visit Aichi to feast your eyes on asari dishes and see how they're cultivated using suspended cages. How about diving for some giant asari!

programme.title: NHK NEWS 7
programme.desc: NEWS7 is NHK's flagship domestic evening news program, bringing you the latest from our Tokyo studio. It's presented with English audio on NHK World for our international viewers.

programme.title: Ethical Every Day
programme.desc: . Rising temperatures are changing marine life. Sushi chains introduce unfamiliar fish, and researchers seek to breed species that can endure higher temperatures. This time, living with a warming sea.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Otani Akira's book "The Night of Baba Yaga" won the UKs prestigious Dagger prize for crime fiction in translation-a first for a Japanese author. She shares the inspiration behind her work. Gene Otani.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . Yoshida Ryusei, an October matsuri, features rockets called ryusei that are made by local teams and carry community prayers high in the sky. Team members pour heart and soul into their efforts.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . In the Edo period, childbirth was a community affair, with the mother-to-be attended by a midwife and the women in her life. It was also characterized by unique customs and superstitions.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.desc: . Gary Tegler, Takuji Takahashi, Ikuko Takeda, Sumie Nagakusa, Tomoko Eri, Toshiaki Nagakusa, Kokei Eri, Yoshiaki Dobuchi.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo

Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Otani Akira's book "The Night of Baba Yaga" won the UKs prestigious Dagger prize for crime fiction in translation-a first for a Japanese author. She shares the inspiration behind her work. Gene Otani.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . The highlight of Onabare is nerikomi, which involves the careful wielding of a long, heavy pole. This follows the parade of a portable shrine through the streets with performances along the way.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . A group of anglers stand in a fast-moving, undulating river at the foot of a rugged cliff. They use "drifting mosquito hooks" to fish for a summer delicacy that was a favorite of the Edo townspeople.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: . On New Year's Eve, families in northeast Japan are visited by monster-like figures called namahage. We follow a group of men trying to keep up their proud tradition as the local population dwindles. Soness Stevens, Hiroyuki Hamada, Koji Okubo, Yusuke Ito.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: . Our bodies are home to trillions of bacteria that influence our physical health and mental well-being. We explore Japan's relationship with fermented foods and "chokatsu" or gut-related activities. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.title: Asia Insight
programme.desc: . The exile of President Marcos Sr. was hailed as a victory for democracy in the Philippines, but with the election of his son 36 years later, shifting views affect how history may be perceived. Vinay Murthy, Naoki Nomura, Haruo Sakitsu.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Imperial Palace
programme.desc: At the Imperial Palace, thoughtful preparation and meticulous attention to detail contribute to a warm welcome for distinguished guests. Join us as we go behind the scenes.

programme.title: Seasoning The Seasons
programme.desc: . This program visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history. Tomohiro Nakano.

programme.title: Document 72 Hours
programme.desc: . On Tsushima Island in southwestern Japan, a small store stocks fresh produce and everyday items and delivers to homes, restaurants, schools and more. Join us for three days on the island. Masako Nishijima.

programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

who include her twin sister Sayaka
are aiming for the gold at the

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: The entire Earth is affected by climate change, occurring with a greater speed and scale than ever before. This program uses scientific data and the latest findings to raise awareness on its impact.

programme.title: Chatroom Japan
programme.desc: . A Nepalese nursing home manager in Fukuoka Prefecture harnesses his background in IT and his diverse staff to give elderly residents and their families peace of mind.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: . Today's theme: Bodies. As AI, digital and virtual spaces take over, thinking about physical bodies is more important than ever. Explore the role of designs which are rooted in human physicality.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Frontrunners
programme.desc: . Fukuoka's summer festival, Hakata Gion Yamakasa, features huge floats decorated with elaborate dolls. We meet Nakamura Hiromine, a fourth-generation doll maker fusing this tradition with modern ideas.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.desc: . The exile of President Marcos Sr. was hailed as a victory for democracy in the Philippines, but with the election of his son 36 years later, shifting views affect how history may be perceived. Vinay Murthy, Naoki Nomura, Haruo Sakitsu.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. who include her twin sister Sayaka
are aiming for the gold at the

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a variety of short programs focusing on Japan and Asia.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . For a time during the Edo period, fireworks lit up the skies practically every night in the summer. It was a chance for women to dress up and let loose ... and grab the attention of male suitors.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.desc: . Japanese researchers have been uncovering previously unknown effects of vitamin C in the body. We report on their latest findings, such as the power to strengthen the bones and prevent dementia. Erica Angyal, Maxwell Powers.

programme.title: The Imperial Palace
programme.desc: At the Imperial Palace, thoughtful preparation and meticulous attention to detail contribute to a warm welcome for distinguished guests. Join us as we go behind the scenes.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: . Today's theme: Bodies. As AI, digital and virtual spaces take over, thinking about physical bodies is more important than ever. Explore the role of designs which are rooted in human physicality.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a wide range of programs from news, current affairs, documentaries, etc. focusing on Japan and Asia.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.desc: . The exile of President Marcos Sr. was hailed as a victory for democracy in the Philippines, but with the election of his son 36 years later, shifting views affect how history may be perceived. Vinay Murthy, Naoki Nomura, Haruo Sakitsu.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Chatroom Japan
programme.desc: . A Nepalese nursing home manager in Fukuoka Prefecture harnesses his background in IT and his diverse staff to give elderly residents and their families peace of mind.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: . Documentary looking at how Japanese craftsmanship has created inventions that are now used and loved around the world. Peter Von Gomm, Yuki Rhinehart, Jason David Danielson, Koki Ando, Toru Iwatani, Shigetaka Kurita, Yoshihiko Akimoto.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: . Our bodies are home to trillions of bacteria that influence our physical health and mental well-being. We explore Japan's relationship with fermented foods and "chokatsu" or gut-related activities. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Documentary 360
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN's flagship documentary series offers must-see episodes on politics, the economy, social trends, science and the environment in Japan and beyond.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Perfect 2hour Guide
programme.desc: A selection of acclaimed programs from NHK's domestic channels dubbed into English.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Professionals
programme.desc: . This doctor can change the future of lung cancer, the cancer that causes the most deaths. We follow him as he pursues new cancer treatment at the head of a team of the world's best in Toronto, Canada. Brendan Fraser, Stevel Marc, Elena Anaya, Kai Luke Brümmer, Suraya Rose Santos.

programme.title: Dining with the Chef mini
programme.desc: . Traditional techniques and resourceful recipes in just 5 minutes! Chefs Saito and Rika present their unique approaches to cooking delicious Japanese food.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: . Today's theme: Bodies. As AI, digital and virtual spaces take over, thinking about physical bodies is more important than ever. Explore the role of designs which are rooted in human physicality.

programme.title: Story Land
programme.desc: Leading actors take turns as solo storytellers to act and recite traditional folk tales loved in Japan for centuries. Watch out, too, for the delightful illustrations.

programme.title: Texico
programme.desc: , Episode 2. Learn the principles of programming without a computer. Animated characters provide fun and lucid explanations of 5 core processes: analysis, combination, generalization, abstraction, and simulation.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: JAPANESE FIREWORKS TRUTH
programme.desc: Japanese fireworks have long enchanted people with their spherical shape and range of colors. We trace the rise of fireworks in Japan and also examine the 400-year-old mystery of the senko hanabi.

programme.title: Hit the Road 5min
programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a variety of short programs focusing on Japan and Asia.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . Centuries ago, Tokyo was known as EDO. More than a million people enjoyed life in this city, they live on in ukiyo-e woodblock prints. Take a deep dive into ukiyo-e and explore the soul of old Tokyo.

programme.title: GRAND SUMO Highlights
programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from March 10, Day 3 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Osaka.

programme.title: Cinematic Journey
programme.desc: km trail following the Pacific Coast in northern Japanwhere he discovers local lifestyles, stunning nature, traces of the area's 2011 disaster, and resilient people.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: Bustling Scenes of Old Tokyo
programme.desc: With its splendid branches and white plum blossoms, this famous painting is known for captivating Van Gogh. Discover why people were able to tell this was a specific tree at Kameido Umeyashiki.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: . Find out about Japan's emergency food options that meet the needs of various people. Also discover the rolling stock method, a smart way to stockpile emergency food.

programme.title: Design museum Japan
programme.desc: . Morinaga Kunihiko (Fashion designer)visits Amami Oshima, Kagoshima. Priestesses on Amami wore patchwork robes with triangular butterfly designs, intended to protect the wearer from unseen spirits.

programme.title: Kabuki Kool
programme.desc: . Actor Kataoka Ainosuke explores "The Battles of Coxinga" by Chikamatsu Monzaemon. It's an epic tale with a Sino-Japanese hero taking up arms in China for the family his father once served.
